title: Student Welfare: Crisis Intervention
description: This policy governs how the school district responds to crises involving students, including situations that may pose a safety threat to individuals or the broader school community. It applies to students, school staff, and the teams responsible for assessing and responding to potential threats or emergencies. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it outlines the structured processes schools use to identify and address safety concerns early, helping to keep students and campuses safe.

timeline.summary: Policy FFB (Local) is being updated to add requirements related to the multidisciplinary threat assessment and safe and supportive team. Key additions include: (1) ensuring team members have expertise in specific areas, (2) adding parental notification and participation requirements before and after threat assessments, and (3) referencing Education Code 37 in the threat assessment process.
changes_added: The Superintendent of the district shall ensure, to the greatest extent practicable, that the members appointed to each team have expertise in counseling, behavior management, mental health and substance use, classroom instruction, special education, school administration, school safety and security, emergency management, and law enforcement.
and Education Code 37 (added to Threat Assessment Process section)
a. Notifying the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student regarding the assessment.
b. Providing an opportunity for the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student to participate in the assessment, either in person or remotely.
c. Providing an opportunity for the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student to submit to the team information regarding the student.
4. After completing a threat assessment of a student, providing to the parent of or person standing in parental relation to the student the team's findings and conclusions regarding the student.

If the student at risk of suicide also makes a threat of violence to others, the team shall conduct a threat assessment in addition to actions taken in accordance with the district's suicide prevention program.
using or possessing tobacco, drugs, or alcohol
